_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House HD149-150 + FL Panhandle SD1/HD1/HD2. Vo lost 2026 D runoff (status:lost). Andrade cross-party deviation: 2019 LGBT non-discrimination cosponsor.

hubert-vo@TX.notes_set: TX House HD149 (Harris Co/Alief), Democrat, in office since 2005. LOST the May 26, 2026 Democratic primary RUNOFF to Darlene Breaux (had tied her by 3 votes in March 3 primary); will not return after term ends ~Jan 2027. Documented anti-conservative 2025 (89th Lege) record per Texas Values Action (9%), Texas Alliance for Life (42%), Texas Eagle Forum (16%): voted AGAINST SB33 (no taxpayer abortion funds), SCR19 (Life Monument), HB229 (What is a Woman Act), SB2 (school choice/ESA), SB13 (ban obscene library books), SB10 (Ten Commandments), SB11 (prayer in schools), SB965 (employee prayer). 2021/2023 individual roll calls (SB8, HB1927, SB1) were caucus-pattern only and are left null per evidence-first discipline. No cross-party deviations found.

1.note: Voted AGAINST SB33 (No Taxpayer Funds for Abortion) and SCR19 (Texas Life Monument), 2025; Texas Alliance for Life 42%.

2.note: Voted AGAINST HB229 (What is a Woman Act), 2025.

0.note: Voted AGAINST SB2 (school choice / Education Savings Accounts), 2025.

3.note: Voted AGAINST SB13 (ban harmful/obscene books in school libraries), 2025.
Voted AGAINST SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms), SB11 (Prayer in Schools), and SB965 (Coach Joe Kennedy employee prayer), 2025.

valoree-swanson@TX.notes_set: TX House HD150 (NW Harris Co/Spring-Tomball), Republican, founding Texas House Freedom Caucus member, in office since 2017; running for re-election March 3, 2026 primary. Texas Right to Life 93% (89th, 2025), Pro-Life Hero 2017, NARAL 0%; Texas Values Action 100% 'Faith & Family Champion'. AUTHORED HB25 (2021 Save Women's Sports) + 2023 college-sports version; voted FOR SB14 (minor gender ban), HB229 (What is a Woman), SB1257 (Detransitioners), SB2 (ESA), SB12 (Parents' Bill of Rights), SB13 (library books), SB10/SB11/SB965 (Ten Commandments/prayer), HB1056 (constitutional money), SB1070 (ERIC withdrawal), HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act), HB9 (2021 border security), HB1927 (2021 permitless carry), HB2837 (block firearm merchant-code tracking). Quote: 'There is never a time that abortion should be allowed.' No progressive deviations; within-coalition: voted NO on HJR98 Article V con-con (Eagle-Forum-aligned position).

0.note: Quote 'There is never a time that abortion should be allowed'; filed measure to revoke abortion-doctor licenses; Texas Right to Life 93% (2025), Pro-Life Hero 2017.

1.note: Voted FOR SB8 (2021 Heartbeat Act) and SB8 (2025 Women's Privacy Act); stated no exception position; filed license-revocation bill.

4.note: NARAL Pro-Choice Texas 0%; pro-life champion (TRTL 93%) — no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

2.note: AUTHORED HB25 Save Women's Sports (2021) + 2023 college version; voted FOR SB14 (minor gender ban), HB229 (What is a Woman), SB1257 (Detransitioners).

4.note: Opposes LGBT curriculum promotion in schools (stated); voted AGAINST HB1738 repeal of Homosexual Conduct statute; Equality Texas grade F.

0.note: Voted FOR SB2 (2025 school choice / Education Savings Accounts).

1.note: Voted FOR SB12 (2025 Parents' Bill of Rights).

2.note: Voted FOR SB13 (ban harmful books in school libraries); opposes LGBT curriculum.

3.note: Voted FOR SB13 restricting obscene/harmful material in school libraries (sexualized content to minors).
Voted FOR SB10 (Ten Commandments), SB11 (Prayer in Schools), SB965 (Coach Joe Kennedy employee prayer), 2025.

0.note: Voted FOR HB1056 (2025 Constitutional Money) establishing gold/silver as legal tender — supports decentralized/cash alternative tender.
Voted FOR HB796 (2025 Texas Sovereignty Act), an anti-commandeering process to challenge unconstitutional federal acts.

2.note: Voted FOR HB2837 (2023) blocking firearm-purchase tracking via merchant category codes — resisting federal/financial gun surveillance.

3.note: Texas Sovereignty Act (HB796) affirms state sovereignty against unconstitutional federal action.

0.note: Voted FOR HB9 (2021) $1.8B border-security/Operation Lone Star appropriation; stated strong support for border wall.
Voted FOR HB1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry); authored HB121 (2019) expanding gun rights.

1.note: Voted FOR HB2837 (2023) blocking de-facto gun registry via financial merchant-code tracking.

don-gaetz@FL.notes_set: FL Senate District 1 (Panhandle: Escambia/Santa Rosa/Okaloosa/Walton), Republican; former Senate President (2012-14), RETURNED to Senate Nov 2024; term ends 2028 (not up in 2026). Chairs Ethics & Elections. IMPORTANT: out of office 2016-Nov 2024, so most signature FL conservative bills (SB300 6-week ban 2023, HB1557 2022, HB1 ESA 2023, HB543 carry 2023, SB1718 2023) PREDATE his return and are left null per evidence-first discipline. Current-term HARD evidence: sponsored SB318 (2025 ESA/school-choice fix, 'Florida was first state to pass universal school choice...we stand firmly'); voted YES on SB 2-C (2025C immigration enforcement package creating Office of State Immigration Enforcement + ICE cooperation); sponsored SB52 (church armed-security, framed as 'expanding gun rights') and filed campus-carry bill removing gun-free zones; sponsored SB7016 tightening constitutional-amendment petition process. DEVIATION (intra-party, not scored): as Ethics & Elections chair led Senate rejection of several DeSantis nominees; co-sponsored SB52 with a Democrat. Thin record by design — scored only documented current-term positions.

0.note: Sponsored SB318 (2025) ESA/school-choice accountability fix; quote 'Florida was the first state to pass universal school choice, because we stand firmly...parents know best'; former Okaloosa County school superintendent.

1.note: Voted YES on SB 2-C (2025C 'TRUMP Act' immigration package), 3rd reading Feb 13, 2025 — maximal state deportation/ICE-cooperation enforcement.

2.note: SB 2-C created the Office of State Immigration Enforcement and ICE-cooperation grants (anti-sanctuary enforcement).

4.note: Sponsored SB52 (church armed-security, self-described 'expanding gun rights') and filed campus-carry bill removing gun-free zones — opposes citizen-disarmament/gun-free-zone initiatives.

michelle-salzman@FL.notes_set: FL House District 1 (Escambia/Pensacola, Panhandle), Republican, in office since Nov 2020; running for re-election 2026. Endorsed by Florida Family Action, NRA 'A' rating. Voted YES on HB5 (2022 15-week ban; gave personal floor testimony), SB300 (2023 6-week ban), HB7; HB1557 (Don't Say Gay), HB1421 (gender-clinic restriction) + 2023 trans-care/bathroom package; co-introduced HB1 (2023 universal ESA); sponsored HB1129 (2024 harm-to-minors online protections); voted YES HB999 (sound money), HB1645 (removing climate-change from statute), SB1718 (2023 immigration/E-Verify) + SB2-B (2025 TRUMP Act); co-sponsored HB543 (permitless carry); PRIME SPONSOR of HB759 (2025, lowering gun age to 18, repealing post-Parkland restriction — passed House 78-34). To the RIGHT of leadership on guns. DEVIATIONS (noted, not scored as policy): publicly clashed with Gov. DeSantis over HB1321 (2025 higher-ed transparency) — he called her 'behaving like Democrats'; voted NO on HB1205 (2025 petition restrictions); held a pre-office advisory role with Equality Florida (biographical — her in-office votes are GOP-aligned).

0.note: Voted YES on HB5 (2022 15-week ban) and SB300 (2023 6-week Heartbeat ban); gave personal floor testimony Feb 16, 2022 supporting the ban.

4.note: Endorsed by Florida Family Action; consistent pro-life voting record — no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

2.note: Voted YES on HB1421 (2023 gender clinical-intervention restriction) and the 2023 trans-care/bathroom sex-designation package.

4.note: Voted YES on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay'), restricting LGBTQ instruction in K-3.

0.note: Co-introduced HB1 (2023 universal Family Empowerment/ESA voucher expansion) and voted YES.

1.note: Voted on the restricting side of SB254 (2023) / HB1421 minor gender-care — parental authority over minor medical/gender interventions.

2.note: Voted YES on HB1557 (Parental Rights in Education) restricting SOGI instruction in early grades.

3.note: Sponsored HB1129 (2024) relating to harm to minors / online protections.

0.note: Voted YES on HB999 (2025) establishing gold/silver as legal tender — decentralized/cash alternative tender.

4.note: Voted YES on HB1645 (2024) removing 'climate change' references from state law — opposing ESG/climate-agenda capture of state policy.

1.note: Voted YES on SB2-B (2025 'TRUMP Act' immigration enforcement overhaul; House passed 82-30).

2.note: Voted YES on SB1718 (2023) and SB2-B (2025) state immigration enforcement (anti-sanctuary).

3.note: Voted YES on SB1718 (2023) mandating E-Verify for businesses with 25+ employees.

1.note: NRA 'A' rating — consistent opposition to red-flag/magazine/AWB/registry gun-control measures.

4.note: PRIME SPONSOR of HB759 (2025) lowering firearm-purchase age to 18, repealing the post-Parkland restriction — opposing citizen disarmament of 18-20-year-olds. Quote: 'the right to defend yourself, the right to keep and bear arms.'

alex-andrade@FL.notes_set: FL House District 2 (Escambia/Santa Rosa, Pensacola/Pace, Panhandle), Republican, attorney; TERM-LIMITED — four terms (elected 2018), leaving after 2026, cannot run for HD2 again. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (high-value catch): in 2019 he was a Republican COSPONSOR of the Florida Competitive Workforce Act, the LGBT non-discrimination bill adding sexual orientation AND gender identity/expression as protected classes (Equality Florida press release) — and on iVoterGuide he DECLINED to personally define marriage as one man/one woman, only defending others' right to that belief. This downgrades the biblical-marriage category (prior scorecard over-credited it at 97/100). Otherwise conservative: voted YES HB5/SB300 (abortion bans), HB1557 (Don't Say Gay), co-introduced HB1 (ESA), co-introduced HB543 (permitless carry), YES HB759 (gun age 18), SB1718 (immigration/E-Verify), HB1451 (deny ID to illegal migrants), HB999 (sound money); NRA-PVF endorsed; iVoterGuide 'life begins at conception...until natural death,' 'protect the freedom of Christians to share the Gospel,' supports border wall. Also led the 2025 House Hope Florida investigation (anti-corruption oversight of a $10M Medicaid-settlement diversion) — a 'fierce DeSantis critic'; that oversight is a public-integrity positive but doesn't map to the rubric cells, so it is noted not scored.

0.note: Voted YES on HB5 (2022 15-week) and SB300 (2023 6-week) abortion bans; iVoterGuide: 'Human life begins at conception and deserves legal protection at every stage.'

3.note: iVoterGuide: legal protection of life 'at every stage until natural death' — opposing euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: Pro-life record; no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

1.note: In 2019 cosponsored the Florida Competitive Workforce Act adding LGBT protected classes in law — contrary to opposing legal recognition of same-sex relationships.

2.note: The 2019 Competitive Workforce Act he cosponsored added 'gender identity or expression' as a protected class — contrary to rejecting transgender ideology in law.

4.note: Voted YES on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay').

0.note: Co-introducer of HB1 (2023 universal ESA/school-choice expansion).

2.note: Voted YES on HB1557 restricting SOGI instruction in early grades.

0.note: iVoterGuide: pledges to 'protect the freedom of Christians to share the Gospel' and give religious freedom 'the highest level of protection.'
Voted YES on HB999 (2025 Constitutional Money) establishing gold/silver as legal tender.
iVoterGuide: supports border wall and merit-based immigration system.

1.note: Voted YES on HB1451 (2024 denying ID cards to illegal migrants) and SB1718 (2023 enforcement with transport penalties).

2.note: Voted YES on SB1718 (2023) omnibus immigration enforcement (anti-sanctuary).

3.note: SB1718 (2023) mandates E-Verify; voted YES (House passed 83-36).

1.note: NRA-PVF endorsed; voted YES HB759 (2025 lowering gun age) — opposes gun-control restrictions.

4.note: Voted YES on SB214 (2023 Second Amendment Financial Privacy Act) blocking firearm-purchase merchant-code tracking — opposing de-facto registry/surveillance.
